muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET / Index()" -}}
<button data-on-click="{{ (.Actions.UpdateUser 42).JS }}">save</button>
<a data-on-click="{{ ((.Actions.UpdateUser 7).OpenWhenHidden true).JS }}">defer</a>
{{- end -}}
{{- define "PATCH /users/{id} UpdateUser(ctx, id)" -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

func (Server) Index() any { return nil }

func (Server) UpdateUser(ctx context.Context, id int) any { return nil }
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestActions(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
	}
	body := rec.Body.String()
	// &#39; is the html-escaped single quote; the browser decodes it back to '
	// so the DOM attribute value is @patch('/users/42').
	for _, want := range []string{
		`data-on-click="@patch(&#39;/users/42&#39;)"`,
		`@patch(&#39;/users/7&#39;, {openWhenHidden: true})`,
	} {
		if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
			t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", body, want)
		}
	}
}
